top_5_urgent_risks: Authenticated shell pages are publicly renderable and rely on client-side auth redirects only.
Web auth stores Supabase session tokens in browser localStorage through default supabase-js persistence.
Extension session tokens are persisted in chrome.storage.local instead of remaining background-only.
Extension logout does not revoke the underlying Supabase session or refresh token.
Auth-adjacent signup and handoff flows lack robust shared rate limiting.

findings.title: Require server-side auth on authenticated shell pages
evidence: app/routes/shell.py:75-157 renders all authenticated shell pages without an auth dependency
app/static/js/app_shell/boot.js:9-38 handles signed-out state client-side after page render
tests/test_phase7_frontend_shell.py:84-97 currently asserts 200 for /dashboard and /editor
findings.exploit_path: Any unauthenticated client can request the protected shell routes directly and receive rendered HTML; only the browser runtime later decides to redirect.
findings.fix_direction: Add server-side auth enforcement before rendering authenticated shell pages and update the shell tests to expect protected behavior.

findings.title: Stop persisting web auth tokens in localStorage
evidence: app/static/js/auth.js:82-92 creates supabase-js client with default storage options
app/templates/auth.html:466-480 signs in with Supabase JS and immediately uses the returned session in the browser
findings.exploit_path: A web XSS bug or malicious extension with page access can read the persisted Supabase session from localStorage and replay bearer/refresh tokens.
findings.fix_direction: Replace default browser persistence with a safer server-managed or non-localStorage session model.

findings.title: Remove extension session tokens from shared extension storage
evidence: extension/background/auth/session_store.ts:18-42 reads and writes the normalized session, including refresh token, to chrome.storage.local
findings.exploit_path: Because tokens live in shared extension storage, session authority is no longer confined to the background worker and can be accessed or overwritten by other privileged extension surfaces.
findings.fix_direction: Keep refresh-capable credentials in a background-only custody model rather than chrome.storage.local.

findings.title: Revoke Supabase sessions on extension logout
evidence: extension/background/handlers/auth_handler.ts:97-101 only clears local state on logout
extension/background/auth/session_manager.ts:34-38 only removes local storage/alarm state
findings.exploit_path: If the refresh token was previously copied, the attacker can continue refreshing the session after the user clicks Sign Out in the extension because server-side session semantics were never invalidated.
findings.fix_direction: Call Supabase sign-out/revocation during extension logout and verify the old refresh token no longer works.

findings.title: Throttle signup and harden auth-sensitive rate limiting
evidence: app/modules/identity/routes.py:37-39 exposes signup with no rate-limit dependency
app/modules/extension/service.py:245-359 and 529-546 rate-limit auth-sensitive flows only through app.state.rate_limiter
app/core/security.py:92-151 implements an in-memory limiter only
findings.exploit_path: Bots can hammer signup directly, and distributed requests can bypass in-memory per-process counters on handoff/auth-sensitive flows by spreading across workers.
findings.fix_direction: Attach explicit auth-sensitive rate limiting to signup and move handoff throttling onto shared infrastructure.

findings.title: Maintain explicit-origin CORS
evidence: app/core/config.py:80-86 rejects wildcard CORS in staging/prod
app/core/security.py:153-165 installs CORSMiddleware with configured origins only

findings.title: Preserve ownership-scoped entity access
evidence: app/modules/common/ownership.py:25-66 loads owned document/note/quote rows by user_id
app/modules/common/relation_validation.py:92-143 validates owned relation ids before replace-all RPCs
tests/test_phase4_workflow_services.py covers multiple foreign-reference rejection paths
findings.fix_direction: Keep centralized ownership validation and relation-RPC tests.

findings.title: Keep redirect validation strict
evidence: app/core/security.py:57-77 rejects external and malformed redirect paths
app/modules/extension/service.py:216-240 blocks nested redirects and non-editor/dashboard targets
tests/test_security_helpers.py:67-79 covers safe/unsafe redirect cases
findings.fix_direction: Reuse the same validators for future callback flows.

findings.title: Keep webhook verification mandatory
evidence: app/modules/billing/service.py:364-385 verifies Paddle signatures before payload processing
findings.fix_direction: Preserve the current signature-first processing order.

findings.title: Dependency review found no critical/high JS advisories
evidence: npm audit --omit=dev returned zero vulnerabilities
root JS dependency set is limited to dev-only TypeScript
findings.fix_direction: Add Python advisory scanning in CI to strengthen this coverage.
